what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record that links a channel-memory attachment ID to a specific image asset in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML metadata block with no body content
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to design-source/capital-raise/p-16
attachment_type is the plural 'images' for a single target
conventions: Channel-scoped asset reference via URI target
Separation of metadata file from the actual image blob
concerns: No content beyond metadata; purpose of the image is not described
Target URI cannot be verified from this file alone
No added_by, caption, mime_type, or version fields
attachment_type plural naming may imply collection semantics that are not evident
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub that points the design-source channel at image capital-raise/p-16, recorded on 2026-06-22.
questions: What visual content does p-16 represent?
Who or what process created/added this attachment?
How does this attachment relate to other att-* files or channel messages?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose statement is present; the schema fields match an image attachment record.
intention_signals: To record that a specific image asset (capital-raise/p-16) is attached to the design-source channel as a referenceable artifact.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address a channel-scoped asset and a plural attachment_type ('images') for a single target.
contribution: A timestamped, typed pointer from a channel-memory attachment ID to a specific image asset, enabling retrieval and grouping of design-source images by attachment ID and channel.
summary_for_embedding: YAML attachment metadata linking attachment ID att-edf2fd25 in the design-source channel to image asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-16, with type images and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and reference an image attachment associated with the design-source channel for a capital-raise visual (p-16).
